item.dc:creator: Skullwriter

item.title: Dr Eugene Lazowski - a man who proved that you don’t have to be a soldier to confound your enemy in a war. He will be remembered as a hero of the highest order.
item.description: Eugene Lazowski, a Polish doctor and member of the resistance, devised a daring medical deception to protect thousands from Nazi persecution. Using a discovery by his colleague Stanisław Matulewicz, Lazowski injected healthy individuals with a strain of Proteus bacteria that caused them to test positive for typhus without actually contracting the disease. Since the Germans feared typhus as a highly contagious and deadly illness, they quarantined entire villages rather than deporting residents to concentration camps. This strategic deception saved an estimated 8,000 people, including Jews in hiding, from certain death. Beyond his medical ruse, Lazowski secretly provided treatment and supplies to Jews confined in a nearby ghetto, risking execution under Nazi law. His efforts were part of a broader resistance movement in occupied Poland, where individuals defied Nazi rule through covert operations. After the war, Lazowski emigrated to the United States, where he continued his medical career and documented his experiences in his memoir 'Prywatna Wojna' (My Private War). His legacy remains a testament to the power of ingenuity and courage in the face of oppression.
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.title: Oops! (I did it again) All memes!
item.description:
This'a here's thee finest collection'a shee'it you ever did see.
This side of the Mississip

Ran out of scrolls on the memaverse!
So now you all get to enjoy my left overs from several consecutive life sentences ago.
ENJOY IDIOTS!
Well slap my ass and call me susie!
Hope you've all had a fine whatever day of the whenever this is!

Man Tax: Jose de la Torre “I have heard there are troubles of more than one kind. Some come from ahead and some come from behind. But I've bought a big bat. I'm all ready you see. Now my troubles are going to have troubles with me!” ― Dr. Seuss
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.title: Life gives you lemons...
item.description: Not a long story, but I spent about 8 months visiting Drs. and dealing with rando skin rashes across my arms back and chest. The last doctor said I had cholinergic urticaria, or hives from sweating. Problem was I didn't get them when I was sweating, it wasn'tconsistent. Fast forward to about two weeks ago, another rando breakout. I hadn't done or eaten anything all morning, except coffee... mmm... Cut coffee out for a full week and notice my symptoms going away. Tried a cup of coffee about a week later and sure enough about 2 hours later starting breaking out and getting itchy again. Take a week off, symptoms got better. Ate a candy bar and broke out again. Feel like I'm at a low spot in my life, and now to lose my emotional support coffee sucks. Could be worse though, so cheers for tonight, I'll be having some wine.
item.dc:creator: digitreal

item.title: Just some boys in Kansas( who are cowards)
item.description:
FP EDIT: First and foremost, nobody is saying being lgbtqia is a bad thing. That is certainly not what is being insinuated here. The song was used because Nazis in the video are insecure and absolutely detest being compared to the awesome community that is the lgbtqia. Attacking their insecurities to me is icing on the cake. Calling any of these Nazis men would be disrespectful to anybody who calls himself a man.( they are children throwing a tantrum because they're scared.) I am sorry that this is a tiktok, but I thought it was hilarious. Make sure you have sound on.
SQUIRREL TAX One or two more weeks and squirrel should be able to go to the wild. She's getting quite strong and energetic

item.title: Grunt! [OC]
item.description:
Maybe it’s just my gym- but good heavens, are the boys okay?? Why are we yelling on the leg press??? If it’s too heavy just lower the weights, you’ll live I promise :,D I felt like I had entered a zoo at feeding time
Also I just wanted to shout out these AMAZING people! They’re the heroes that allow me to afford making these comics for everyone <3
Cat tax!! It’s Beany-Bean, freshly awakened from a nap

item.title: What Happened Today #115
item.description: ⚖️? The scales tip and masks slip as Trump's authoritarian drift accelerates and weaponizes civil power. He's still demanding Harvard turn over student names despite federal court orders, recruiting park visitors to snitch on history displays that don't fit his view, and constantly attacking the Fed chair he appointed. America is dying because of steady pressure on institutions, rewriting of history, and punishment of dissent until resistance becomes unthinkable. We have news on how you can pick up some civic training this week. Be sure to sign up for Tuesday's Indivisible call. Meanwhile, don't rely on the algorithm. Let me send this free and private summary to you every night so that you never miss what's going on. Add your email on the homepage of GovBrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist Important: I post on social media because many people find it more convenient, but the May stats I calculated earlier today showed that there is a steady decline in how often GovBrief is seen on its three biggest social channels. I'll keep posting (try and stop me!), but strongly encourage you to sign up for the newsletter or simply follow us on Substack as a backup. Reddit could cancel politics or Instagram could lean in more to video content. There's no guarantee that you'll see this on your socials even if you do follow GBT there. I remember doing this with Heather Cox Richardson's posts when she started in earnest years ago. I just don't want to lose you, Imgur! 1. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-rebukes-putin 2. https://go.govbrief.today/us-africa-message 3. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-delays-eu-tariffs 4. https://go.govbrief.today/powell-speech-princeton 5. https://go.govbrief.today/johnson-state-snap 6. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-harvard-foreign-students 7. https://go.govbrief.today/internior-negative-america Fighting Back - https://go.govbrief.today/indivisible-training-may27 Yesterday's Top Story - https://go.govbrief.today/indivisible-training-may27
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1372 (Slavic calvary justice)
item.description:
Let's get this bread. I guess.
Fucking hope so. I'm so tired. Woke up with an anxiety attack. In tears after, for some reason, a dream about Arabic fonts?
The ad for the history teacher is up again so I guess I'm not getting that job
Nobody calls me back except for scam positions
I haven't seen a catbird in an age. I like their song. It's nostalgic for me for a morning by the Animas river in Colorado.
Yeah, this tracks. Honestly? I'm not sure how much longer I can do this. But maybe it's just my extremely high anxiety levels this morning. How do they rise up.
One reason I've always started carrying a knife. If I'm taken, maybe I can at least make them bleed.
It's pretty much canonical thar Jesus was a foot guy, though, if anything; but the Canon Gospels aren't clear enough as to whether Christ, fully Human and fully God, and therefore faced with the same temptations as you or I, would have preferred boobs or arse. I wouldn't be surprised if there was something in one of the gnostic texts. Those guys thought of everything.
This was a bird I was really hoping to see when I was in Greece almost 15 years ago. I mean, who WOULDN'T be excited about a technicolor chickadee. I only saw a great tit though, but I got some great photos outside of Meteora, and now I sound like a pervert.
Could be a good name for a band.
I'm so tired of everything.
I always love these etymology charts.
Or outside the cave. Or something.
Definitely the endless torment.
So sassy I love it.
The ediacran fauna doesn't get the love it deserves.
I'm going to have to cancel the Pando trip, too. I literally have nothing to look forward to right now on the long term except maybe the death of some public figures, but that's hardly enough for morale.
Fighting in the big cities in Helldivers has been fun.
That is a fancy getup.
I haven't seen an ocean in.... 5 years?
If I could, I would. Honestly either of these sound great.
I used to be this kind of birder. Now I'm more into watching behaviors and finding joy in every bird I see.
Scarlet Tanagers in death.
Mood.
Utah petroglyphs
Pippin tax Today's message from Pippin: "thank you. Fuck you. Peekaboo. Hi Pippin. Peekaboo. Piiipppiiiin!"
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: The bloodline stops here
item.description: I wasn't going to have kids anyway, because I don't want them, but it's funny to draw the parallel
item.dc:creator: TheBloodyLady

item.title: and they live happily ever after
item.dc:creator: TheWhatever666

item.title: I really like salsa! :)
item.description: https://www.cooksmarts.com/articles/pepper-types-and-easy-salsa-recipes I really like salsa, but I love hot salsa. It has always annoyed me that Pace sells mild and medium Picante sauce because Picante means "Spicy" and it feels wrong that they sell mild spicy sauce. However, Pace picante is chunky salsa. Conventional salsa is "Restaurant-style," smoother, and pico de gallo is raw chunky salsa. I keep trying to look for a good supplier of hot salsa here in Arizona. I don't want to go through lots of small containers! :)
item.dc:creator: MenloPart
